label: PASS
rationale: After the power cycle, LockOnReset {0} sets WriteLocked true, but the later Set changes WriteLockEnabled to false; the write lock cell is ignored when WriteLockEnabled is false, so the successful write is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The trajectory disables WriteLockEnabled after the power cycle; with WriteLockEnabled false, WriteLocked is ignored, so rejecting the write as INVALID_PARAMETER is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Level 0 Locked is set only for enabled read or write locks. After WriteLockEnabled is cleared and ReadLockEnabled is false, no enabled active lock remains, so Locked=0 is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Level 0 Locked requires an enabled read or write lock. Both effective locks are disabled or disregarded, so reporting Locked=1 is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: LockOnReset {0,3} does not include Hardware reset type 1. The unlocked ReadLocked and WriteLocked cells should therefore be preserved through HardwareReset, matching false,false.

label: FAIL
rationale: Hardware reset is type 1 and is not in LockOnReset {0,3}; after the cells were manually unlocked, HardwareReset should preserve false,false, not relock them.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable is set true, and TPER_RESET applies Programmatic reset type 3; LockOnReset {0,3} requires ReadLocked and WriteLocked to be set true, matching the Get result.

label: FAIL
rationale: With TPER_RESET enabled, Programmatic reset type 3 matches LockOnReset {0,3}, so ReadLocked and WriteLocked should become true; returning false,false is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Power cycle matches LockOnReset {0}, but WriteLockEnabled is false, so WriteLocked is ignored and writes remain allowed; the successful write is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: Power cycle matches LockOnReset {0} and ReadLockEnabled is true, so ReadLocked becomes meaningful and true; a read from the range should not succeed.
